article_title: Los Alamos THOR Framework Uses Tensor Networks to Solve a Century-Old Physics Problem 400 Times Faster Than Supercomputer Simulations

content_preview.title: Los Alamos THOR Framework Uses Tensor Networks to Solve a Century-Old Physics Problem 400 Times Faster Than Supercomputer Simulations
content_preview.summary: Los Alamos and University of New Mexico researchers built THOR, an open-source tensor network framework that computes configurational integrals for crystalline solids 400 times faster than molecular dynamics simulations.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Predicting how atoms behave inside a material under heat and pressure is one of the foundational problems of statistical physics. The mathematics that governs those predictions, known as the configurational integral, has been understood in principle since the early twentieth century. In practice, evaluating the integral directly has been considered computationally impossible: the number of dimensions scales with the number of atoms in the system, and classical integration techniques would requir...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
2 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent science article with clear explanation of complex computational physics. Good structure progressing from problem to solution to validation to implications.
editor_notes.source_verification: ScienceDaily confirmed researcher names (Alexandrov, Truong), institutions, three test materials, 400x speed improvement, and Physical Review Materials publication. Phys.org confirmed THOR acronym, tensor train cross interpolation technique, Alexandrov quotes, open-source GitHub availability (github.com/lanl/thor). APS journal returned 403 but DOI 10.1103/xrbw-xr49 is a legitimate Physical Review Materials reference. All sources are reputable academic/science outlets.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified: 400x speed improvement, three materials tested (copper, argon, tin), tensor train cross interpolation methodology, researcher names and affiliations, open-source availability.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, accessible science writing. No sensationalism or AI self-references.
editor_notes.originality: No existing THOR framework or tensor network coverage in the archive.
recommendations: journals.aps.org and phys.org are legitimate academic sources that should be added to the allowlist
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong computational physics article with verified claims and excellent explanatory writing. Source access warnings are due to bot-blocking, not source invalidity. Approved.
